blocks.body: Present Continuous: Questions and Short Answers — Am I...?, Is she...?, Are they...? and short answers.

{"level":"A1–A2","intro":"In Present Continuous questions, the form am / is / are moves before the subject, and the main verb stays in -ing.","rules":[{"label":"Am I ...?","color":"emerald","text":"With I, start the question with am.","example":"Am I talking too fast?"},{"label":"Is he / she / it ...?","color":"blue","text":"With he / she / it, use is before the subject.","example":"Is she working now?"},{"label":"Are you / we / they ...?","color":"amber","text":"With you / we / they, use are before the subject.","example":"Are they coming with us?"}]}

{"title":"1. Basic question models","intro":"Questions are built with be, not with do / does.","items":[{"label":"I","title":"Am I + verb-ing?","subtitle":"Am I speaking clearly?"},{"label":"He / She / It","title":"Is he / she / it + verb-ing?","subtitle":"Is he sleeping? / Is it raining?"},{"label":"You / We / They","title":"Are you / we / they + verb-ing?","subtitle":"Are you listening? / Are they waiting outside?"},{"label":"Short Answers","title":"Yes, ... am / is / are.","subtitle":"Yes, I am. / No, she isn't. / Yes, they are."}]}

{"title":"2. How to answer briefly","sections":[{"label":"Yes","color":"emerald","description":"In a positive short answer, repeat the form of be.","examples":[{"en":"Are you studying now? — Yes, I am.","ua":"Are you studying now? — Yes, I am."},{"en":"Is he working today? — Yes, he is.","ua":"Is he working today? — Yes, he is."}]},{"label":"No","color":"blue","description":"In a negative short answer, isn't / aren't are very common.","examples":[{"en":"Is she sleeping? — No, she isn't.","ua":"Is she sleeping? — No, she isn't."},{"en":"Are they waiting? — No, they aren't.","ua":"Are they waiting? — No, they aren't."}]},{"label":"Word order","color":"amber","description":"Compare the statement and the question: the form of be moves before the subject.","examples":[{"en":"She is reading. → Is she reading?","ua":"Inversion with be."},{"en":"They are coming. → Are they coming?","ua":"No do / does."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Question and short answer","intro":"In the answer, do not repeat the whole sentence. A short form is enough.","rows":[{"en":"Am I talking too fast?","ua":"Yes, you are. / No, you aren't.","note":"With Am I...?, the answer is often you are / you aren't."},{"en":"Is she working now?","ua":"Yes, she is. / No, she isn't.","note":"is → she is / she isn't"},{"en":"Are they waiting for us?","ua":"Yes, they are. / No, they aren't.","note":"are → they are / they aren't"}],"warning":"Do not say Do they are waiting? or Yes, she does."}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Adding do / does to Present Continuous.","wrong":"❌ Do they are waiting outside?","right":"✅ Are they waiting outside?"},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Keeping be after the subject like in a statement.","wrong":"❌ She is reading now?","right":"✅ Is she reading now?"},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Using do in a short answer.","wrong":"❌ Yes, she does.","right":"✅ Yes, she is."}]}

{"title":"5. Short summary","items":["A Present Continuous question = am / is / are + subject + verb-ing.","With I, start with Am I...?","With he / she / it, start with Is...?","With you / we / they, start with Are...?","In short answers, repeat the form of be: Yes, I am. / No, she isn't."]}
